string 2: Something doesn't feel right...
{next}What's the matter?
{next}Yeah, you are acting a bit sus.
{end}[I should leave her alone.]

This tip I just got feels sus. But it's more about the
tipster than the tip itself.

Hey, who are you calling sus?! You'd never find anyone as
honest and trustworthy as me!

Hmm... It's just weird. The phrasing makes it sound like the
tipster's involved somehow.

The tip is about a strange cosplayer in front of the Koma
Theater in Higashi-Shinjuku.

The "strange" part, though, is that it says the cosplayer is
a phase electron lifeform.
Who'd send such a tip to me, though?
{next}Inori's dad?
{next}Maybe it was you?
{next}Okay, you got me. It was me.
Yeah, I thought so, too.

Y-You think I'm behind this? Why would I waste my time doing
such a thing?!

Huh? It was you? No way. I'd be able to tell if that was the
case.

I feel like it's someone older. Maybe Inori's pop, or
someone from public safety?
But I wonder why the Digimon is disguised as a human...

At any rate, let's investigate. We can meet up at the Koma
Theater Square in Shinjuku!
Hey, look over there.

Let us fight fairly in the spirit of chivalry! Why are you
laughing?! No pictures!
Urk... Is the art of chivalry dead amongst this crowd?!

He's been doing that for a while, challenging people to
fights... or more like duels?

Everyone thinks it's all in the name of fun, though, so they
just look on in amusement.

You should go start up a conversation. It'll likely lead to
a duel, so be careful.

You seem kind of hesitant. It IS acting pretty strange,
though, for sure...

If you do go talk to it, stay alert, because I have a
feeling something will happen.

You look like a worthy opponent. Would you care to test your
mettle against me?

I challenge you to an honorable duel. Let the battle...
begin!
{next}I accept.
Your costume is amazing.
{end}Wait a second.

You accept my challenge without hesitation! I applaud your
heroic spirit, friend!

This is not a costume! Or perhaps you are trying to gain a
mental advantage over me?

You need time to prepare? Chivalry dictates that I wait. Let
me know when you are ready.
Are you ready? Now, then, I challenge you to a duel!
{next}I accept.
{end}I need a little more time.
Prepare yourself!

I can see you are a true warrior. Simply tell me when you
are ready for our duel, then.

I am testing the chivalry of humans here to see if they are
worthy of King Drasil's orders.

Holding back in battle would be disrespectful. I will give
it my all.

Come and demonstrate to me your chivalrous nature! The
battle begins now!

Ungh... You are a most worthy adversary. Hm? Something is
amiss...

A gate...! It would seem that our epic clash has presented
me with a new opponent.

If it wishes to face me, then I will accept that request.
Let the battle... begin!

Such strength! I do not feel I can handle this one alone. I
am in need of assistance.

Our primary focus must be protecting these bystanders from
harm. Now, we fight as one!
You are quite a formidable fighter. I am impressed.

I feel that a knight such as yourself is worthy of carrying
out King Drasil's orders.
I suppose it is time for me to head to the rendezvous point.

I saw everything! You were great! I got some great footage,
too!

I've been thinking about why Inori's pop would've given me
that tip...
{next}He was testing you?
{next}He wanted to protect Shinjuku?
{next}Maybe he had some free time on his hands?

You think so, too? Yeah, that's the first thing that came to
my mind as well.

Actually, that might've been a part of it. You never know
when something bad'll happen.

That'd be interesting. Still, I think he probably had a
better reason than just that.

He probably gave me that tip as a test to see how I'd
respond to it.

If I didn't do as he expected, he likely had another way
lined up to protect Shinjuku.

Did I do the right thing? It's all conjecture at this point.
Anyway, this is for you.
